metadata.seed: Last week I was on my way to class and my girlfriend actually called me up saying that I forgot to take out the trash. And I kind of started thinking, wow, like, I forgot to take out the trash. Something so simple, a simple chore. All I had to do was take out the trash today and I forgot to do it. And then I started to think, well, I can't do something as simple as that. Why is she still with me? Or, and why, why can't I do that? Why can't I do a simple task? Am I good enough for anyone? Am I good enough for her? Kind of starting to feel a little fearful that she would just up and leave me.,

patient.query: Last week I was on my way to class and my girlfriend actually called me up saying that I forgot to take out the trash. And I kind of started thinking, wow, like, I forgot to take out the trash. Something so simple, a simple chore. All I had to do was take out the trash today and I forgot to do it. And then I started to think, well, I can't do something as simple as that. Why is she still with me? Or, and why, why can't I do that? Why can't I do a simple task? Am I good enough for anyone? Am I good enough for her? Kind of starting to feel a little fearful that she would just up and leave me.,

triggers: Receiving a call from girlfriend about forgetting to take out the trash
automatic_thoughts: I forgot to take out the trash, something so simple.
I can't do something as simple as that.
Why is she still with me?
Am I good enough for anyone?
Am I good enough for her?

What's one thing a friend might say to you right now, knowing how much you're struggling with these thoughts?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I felt this rush of panic at first. I thought about how my partner would react and it made me feel really anxious. It’s like I could already hear them being upset, and then I started questioning if I’m just not good enough to handle things like that. It’s just frustrating, you know?

I can't do something as simple as that.

patient.query: When I think about them being upset, I feel this heavy knot in my stomach. I start imagining all the things they might say or how disappointed they might look. It’s like I can’t escape that vision of failure, and then I worry they’ll just think I’m a mess. It makes me want to pull away rather than face it head-on.

triggers: Receiving a call from girlfriend about forgetting to take out the trash
automatic_thoughts: I forgot to take out the trash, something so simple.
I can't do something as simple as that.
Why is she still with me?
Am I good enough for anyone?
Am I good enough for her?
I can already hear them being upset.
I worry they’ll just think I’m a mess.

Can you tell me a little more about what goes through your mind when you imagine them being disappointed?

patient.query: It’s mostly the same stuff, like, “Why can’t you just be better at this?” or “I can’t believe you didn’t handle it.” It feels so personal, like they’re pointing out all my flaws. I think about how I’ve tried to improve, but then it feels like it doesn’t matter. It just makes me feel small, you know?

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel like they’re attacking me sometimes. It’s not just about the trash; it’s like all my past mistakes come flooding back. I start thinking, “What’s wrong with me that I can’t even get this right?” And then I just shrink back, feeling like I’m never going to be enough for them. It’s exhausting to carry that around.

patient.query: I guess it’s the way they react, you know? Sometimes it feels like their frustration is aimed right at me. It’s not just about what’s happening in the moment; it’s like I can feel their disappointment in everything I’ve ever done wrong. I just want to be seen for who I am now, but instead, it feels like I’m always stuck in this cycle of proving myself.

patient.query: It really is exhausting. I mean, sometimes I just want to scream, “I’m not that person anymore!” But then I worry that they won’t believe me or that they’ll just see me as that failure forever. I feel trapped between wanting to show them I’ve changed and fearing they’ll only remember the old me. It makes it hard to just relax and be myself around them.

I want to be honest about how I’m feeling, but I keep thinking they won’t understand or will just see it as another excuse. I feel so stuck, like I’m juggling all these expectations, and I don’t even know how to drop them.

patient.query: I guess I just have this nagging worry that they’ll think I’m being dramatic or weak. I mean, they’ve always been the strong one, and I worry that if I show my vulnerability, it’ll just reinforce their belief that I can’t handle things. It feels like I’d just be adding more pressure on myself to prove I can be strong, and that’s overwhelming.

patient.query: I think it comes from how they've reacted in the past. Like, whenever I've tried to open up, it felt like they just brushed it off or made me feel silly for feeling that way. It kind of sticks with you, you know? So I guess I just assume they’ll do it again, and it makes me feel like I have to keep everything bottled up. I really don’t want to feel that embarrassment again.
